text: the 'pro' look is honestly just lighting and audio, people will forgive shaky footage but they will click off instantly if the audio sounds like you are recording in a bathroom
wait actually—is lighting really that big a deal for screen recordings? i feel like nobody cares what my face looks like if the software demo is clear
if you are doing face-cam stuff, yes. if it is just screen capture, you are right, nobody cares.
the real mistake people make is trying to script every single word.
omg yes. it sounds like a robot reading the terms and conditions.
bullet points, people. just use bullet points and riff. it sounds way more natural.
sure but like... some people are terrible at riffing. i need the script or i just ramble for 20 minutes about nothing.
then record the script, listen to it, and pretend you are explaining it to a friend while you re-record.
the classic 'teach it to your cat' method. it actually works.
my cat is a harsh critic though.
